meta.title: Claude Code Feature Overview — A User's Perspective
meta.description: Claude Code Feature Overview: From v0.2.21 to v2.1.76, 220+ versions and 1000+ updates fully documented. Covering AI models, multi-Agent collaboration, MCP ecosystem, terminal experience, security sandbox, and more.
meta.keywords: Claude Code, Anthropic, AI Programming, Agent, MCP, CLI, VS Code, SDK
meta.ogTitle: Claude Code Feature Overview — Complete Evolution from v0.2 to v2.1
meta.ogDescription: 220+ versions, 1000+ updates. From terminal to IDE, from solo coding to multi-Agent collaboration — discover every capability of Claude Code.

hero.badge: v0.2.21 — v2.1.76 Full Version Feature Summary
hero.title:
Claude Code
Feature Overview
hero.subtitle:
From terminal to IDE, from solo coding to a team of Agents doing the heavy lifting.
220+ version iterations, 1000+ updates — each feature comes with real-world scenarios so you can hit the ground running.
hero.terminalOutput2: 1M context | agent teams | auto-memory | fast mode | voice | /loop | /effort | elicitation | session naming
hero.terminalTyping: Refactor this module with 3 agents: one for security, one for perf, one for tests

recent.label: 🔥 Recent Highlights
recent.title: Latest Features
recent.subtitle: v2.1.58 — v2.1.76: MCP elicitation lets servers ask you questions mid-task, /effort adjusts reasoning intensity on the fly, -n names sessions at startup, 1M context now default for Max/Team/Enterprise, /color personalizes your prompt bar — more power and more polish.

models.label: ◆ AI Models & Reasoning
models.title: Pick Your Model Like a Tool: Heavy Hammer for Hard Jobs, Swift Blade for Quick Ones
models.subtitle: Opus 4.6 deep reasoning for complex architecture, Fast Mode for instant daily coding, /model to switch — no overthinking needed.

agents.label: ◆ Multi-Agent Collaboration
agents.title: A One-Person Team, N Agents Doing the Work
agents.subtitle: Say 'refactor this module' and three Agents split up: security audit, perf optimization, test writing — you just grab a coffee and wait for the report.

sessions.label: ◆ Sessions & Memory
sessions.title: Close Your Laptop — Claude Remembers Your Code Tomorrow
sessions.subtitle: Auto-memory saves project preferences, --resume picks up yesterday's conversation, /fork explores alternatives — context never gets lost.

devflow.label: ◆ Developer Workflow
devflow.title: From git clone to PR merged — Claude Handles Everything in Between
devflow.subtitle: Read docs, search code, write logic, run tests, submit PR — no window-switching needed, Claude runs the whole pipeline.

mcp.label: ◆ MCP Ecosystem
mcp.title: MCP Connects Everything: Slack to Database, Full Pipeline
mcp.subtitle: Plugins, Skills, Hooks, OAuth — Claude Code isn't an island, it's the hub of your dev toolchain.

terminal.label: ◆ Terminal Experience
terminal.title: Terminal Experience Maxed Out: Vim, CJK Input, 20-Language Voice
terminal.subtitle: CJK wide chars align properly, IME full-width spaces work, Vim keybindings feel silky — every terminal user pain point is addressed.

security.label: ◆ Security & Sandbox
security.title: Security Through Layers: Sandbox + Permissions + Injection Protection
security.subtitle: Worried about AI writing code? Sandbox isolates execution, permissions control every action, enterprise compliance out of the box.

sdk.label: ◆ SDK & Integrations
sdk.title: More Than a CLI — A Complete AI Development Platform
sdk.subtitle: TypeScript/Python SDK for CI/CD, Desktop app out of the box, OTel for full observability — integrate however you want.

performance.label: ◆ Performance
performance.title: Instant Startup, No Lag in Long Sessions — Performance Matters
performance.subtitle: 30+ memory leaks fixed, rendering overhead cut by 74%, 8-hour sessions still silky smooth.

platform.label: ◆ Cross-Platform
platform.title: Windows, macOS, Linux — Runs on Whatever You Use
platform.subtitle: ARM64 native, RHEL 8 compatible, WSL2 image paste fixed — unified experience across different team setups.

vscode.label: ◆ VS Code Integration
vscode.title: Don't Want to Open a Terminal? Use Claude Right in VS Code
vscode.subtitle: Spark icon for session management, Plan view with comments, one-click MCP server management — everything in your IDE.

shortcuts.label: ◆ Shortcuts & Commands
shortcuts.title: The Efficiency Ceiling for Keyboard Warriors
shortcuts.subtitle: Ctrl+B for background tasks, /plan to kick off planning, /loop for scheduled checks, /effort to tune reasoning — hands stay on keyboard, productivity maxed out.

timeline.label: ◆ Version Timeline
timeline.title: From v0.2 to v2.1 — One Year's Work That Others Take Three
timeline.subtitle: 220+ versions, from 'it works' to 'it's great' to 'can't live without it' — the complete evolution story.

footer.advancedGuide: 🚀 Explore More Advanced Claude Code Usage →

0.title: MCP Elicitation — MCP Servers Can Now Ask You Questions
0.desc: v2.1.76 adds MCP elicitation support: MCP servers can pop up an interactive dialog (form fields or browser URL) mid-task to request structured input. New Elicitation and ElicitationResult hooks let you intercept or auto-fill responses. MCP is no longer a one-way street.

1.title: Session Naming — Name Your Sessions, Spot Them Instantly
1.desc: Add -n "refactor auth module" at startup to name your session — it shows live on the prompt bar. Running five windows at once? No more guessing which is which. Since v2.1.75, /rename also shows the name on the prompt bar.

2.title: /effort — Dial Reasoning Intensity on the Fly
2.desc: v2.1.76 adds the /effort slash command to adjust model reasoning intensity mid-conversation. Switch between low / medium / high freely. Use /effort auto to reset to default. No more leaving the chat to change configs.

3.title: v2.1.75 — v2.1.76 Stability Sweep
3.desc: Fixed deferred tools losing input schemas after compaction (array/number parameter type errors), plan mode re-asking for approval after already accepted, voice mode swallowing keypresses during permission dialogs, auto-compaction retrying indefinitely (circuit breaker after 3 attempts), token estimation over-counting causing premature compaction. Background agents now preserve partial results when killed. Model fallback notifications are always visible with human-friendly names.

4.title: /plan Starts in One Line — Capture the Idea While It's Hot
4.desc: v2.1.72 adds an optional description argument to /plan. You can now type /plan fix the auth bug and Claude jumps straight into plan mode with a concrete task. One less round trip while the idea is still fresh.

5.title: /copy Gets a Write Mode — Perfect Over SSH
5.desc: The /copy picker now supports the w key to write the focused selection directly to a file, bypassing the clipboard. On remote boxes, inside tmux, or anywhere clipboard integration is flaky, this feels instantly more usable.

6.title: Effort Levels Get Simpler — ○ ◐ ● at a Glance
6.desc: Effort is now simplified to low / medium / high, shown as ○ ◐ ●, with /effort auto to reset. Instead of guessing whether Claude is thinking hard or staying lightweight, you can tell at a glance.

7.title: Worktree In, Worktree Out — Multi-branch Experiments Feel Cleaner
7.desc: New ExitWorktree pairs with EnterWorktree, making isolated worktree sessions easier to leave cleanly. When agents spin off into side branches, cleanup now feels like part of the workflow instead of an afterthought.

8.title: Config & Voice Feel More Natural
8.desc: /config now uses Escape to cancel, Enter to save and close, and Space to toggle settings. Voice transcription also got better with repo names and dev terms like regex, OAuth, and JSON. Small interaction papercuts, gone.

9.title: v2.1.72 Stability Fixes Highlights
9.desc: Fixes include tool search still working with ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL when ENABLE_TOOL_SEARCH is set, slower exits around background tasks, agent progress stuck on Initializing, /clear killing background tasks, and several permission rule edge cases. Less flash, more day-to-day polish.

10.title: VS Code: Major Upgrade — Your IDE Is All You Need
10.desc: New spark icon in the activity bar shows all sessions at a glance. Plan preview supports full Markdown view with comments. Native MCP server management dialog — no need to switch to the terminal. Prompt re-render reduced by 74%. Compaction preserves images for prompt cache reuse — faster and cheaper.

11.title: /claude-api Skill & Effort Visualization
11.desc: Brand new built-in /claude-api skill for building API applications. Logo and spinner display effort level. Opus 4.6 defaults to medium effort; "ultrathink" enables high effort.

12.title: InstructionsLoaded Hook & Hot Reload
12.desc: InstructionsLoaded hook triggers when CLAUDE.md is loaded. /reload-plugins hot reloads without restart. Hook events support agent_id/agent_type fields. Voice STT expanded to 20 languages.

13.title: HTTP Hooks & /simplify & /batch
13.desc: v2.1.63 adds HTTP hooks — POST JSON to a URL and receive responses. New /simplify (simplify code) and /batch (batch processing) built-in skills. Project config and auto-memory shared across git worktrees.

14.title: Remote Control & /copy Interactive Copy
14.desc: v2.1.58 Remote Control expanded to more users. v2.1.69 supports custom names. /copy interactively selects code blocks; "Always copy full response" for one-click copying. Auto-memory automatically saves useful context.

15.title: v2.1.70 Key Fixes Highlights
15.desc: Fixed ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL causing API 400 errors with third-party gateways. Fixed Windows/WSL clipboard corrupting CJK/emoji text. Fixed Enter inserting newlines on slow SSH connections. Fixed /color command unable to reset to default.

0.title: Opus 4.6 — Flagship Reasoning
0.desc: Officially released in v2.1.32, the "biggest brain" of Claude Code. Ideal for complex architecture decisions, large-scale refactoring, and deep code analysis. Full 1M context window — view entire monorepo modules at once. From v2.1.68, Max/Team defaults to medium effort — enough for most tasks. When you hit a tough problem, just /model to max it out.

1.title: Fast Mode — Blazing Speed
1.desc: Same Opus 4.6 model, faster output. Switch instantly with /fast. The best choice for daily coding and pair programming.

2.title: Sonnet 4.6 — The Balanced Choice
2.desc: Added in v2.1.45. Now supports 1M context window, replacing Sonnet 4.5 as the primary coding model for Max plans. From v2.1.69, Sonnet 4.5 users on Pro/Max/Team Premium are automatically migrated to Sonnet 4.6.

3.title: Haiku 4.5 — Lightweight & Efficient
3.desc: Added to the model selector in v2.0.17. Defaults to Sonnet for plan mode and Haiku for execution (SonnetPlan mode). Available for Pro users. Powers the Explore sub-agent for efficient codebase searches.

4.title: 1M Context Window
4.desc: No more struggling with massive codebases. Both Opus 4.6 and Sonnet 4.6 support 1 million token context. Can be disabled via CLAUDE_CODE_DISABLE_1M_CONTEXT as needed.

5.title: /model Instant Switching
5.desc: Model selector shows human-readable labels and prompts for upgrades when new versions are available. From v2.1.63, the active model is displayed in the slash command menu. Supports <kbd>Alt+P</kbd> to switch models while typing. Opus Plan Mode can be set to use Opus only during the planning phase.

6.title: Extended Thinking & Effort Control
6.desc: Deep thinking mode lets the model reason thoroughly before responding. Triggered by saying "think" or "ultrathink" (v2.1.68 reintroduced the ultrathink keyword for high effort). Set the default via alwaysThinkingEnabled. In v2.1.72, effort is simplified to low / medium / high, shown as ○ ◐ ●, with /effort auto to jump back to the default.

7.title: Model Evolution Timeline
7.desc: v1.0.0 GA launched with Sonnet 4 + Opus 4 → v1.0.69 upgraded to Opus 4.1 → v2.0.51 Opus 4.5 → v2.0.58 Pro users got Opus 4.5 → v2.1.32 Opus 4.6 → v2.1.45 Sonnet 4.6 → v2.1.68 removed Opus 4/4.1 → v2.1.69 auto-migrated Sonnet 4.5 to 4.6.

0.title: Agent Teams — Multi-Agent Parallelism
0.desc: Research preview feature. A single instruction is broken into multiple subtasks, with multiple agents running in parallel in tmux, each completing their work independently. Enable with CLAUDE_CODE_EXPERIMENTAL_AGENT_TEAMS=1. Like having your own AI squad — you just give the orders.

1.title: Background Agent — Fire Tasks and Move On
1.desc: <kbd>Ctrl+B</kbd> sends tasks to the background while you keep working. Get notified when they complete. <kbd>Ctrl+F</kbd> kills all background agents. Long-running bash commands are automatically backgrounded — no more staring at progress bars.

2.title: Agent Isolation — Worktree
2.desc: Declare isolation: worktree in agent definitions to automatically run in isolated git worktrees without interference. --worktree flag starts an isolated session. Supports WorktreeCreate/WorktreeRemove hook events. v2.1.69 adds a worktree field (name, path, branch, and original repo directory) to status line hook commands.

3.title: Custom Agents
3.desc: Since v1.0.60, create custom sub-agents (/agents). Supports model field, memory frontmatter, tools restrictions (Task(agent_type) syntax), permissionMode, and disallowedTools. Use claude agents to list all configurations.

4.title: Explore Sub-Agent
4.desc: A Haiku-powered codebase exploration agent that efficiently searches code to save context. Claude can dynamically select the model for sub-agents and resume previously started sub-agents. v2.1.72 restores the Agent tool model parameter for per-invocation model overrides; team agents now automatically inherit the leader's model — no need to configure each one individually.

5.title: Agent Memory & Hooks
5.desc: Agents support memory frontmatter (persistent memory at user/project/local scope). TeammateIdle and TaskCompleted hook events notify progress. SubagentStart/SubagentStop hooks track lifecycle. v2.1.69 adds agent_id (sub-agent) and agent_type fields to hook events. Terminal title shows agent name when using --agent.

0.title: Auto-Memory & /memory
0.desc: Claude automatically remembers project conventions, code style, and architecture preferences as you work — no need to re-explain next time. Use /memory to manage stored memories. .claude/rules/ stores project rules. From v2.1.63, auto-memory is shared across git worktrees, benefiting all branches of the same repo.

1.title: Session Resume /resume
1.desc: Introduced in v0.2.93 with --continue and --resume. Search by session ID, custom title, or git branch. Initially shows 50 sessions. --from-pr resumes PR-associated sessions. Memory usage reduced by 68%. v2.1.69 /resume selector shows the most recent prompt (instead of the first), fixing some titles displaying as "(session)".

2.title: Session Fork /fork & /rewind
2.desc: Fork a new session from the current conversation to explore different directions. /rewind rolls back the conversation and undoes code changes. Supports custom session IDs. After forking, prompts show how to return to the original session.

3.title: Smart Compaction /compact — Chat Forever Without Worry
3.desc: Auto-compaction introduced in v0.2.47 — theoretically unlimited conversations. v2.0.64 made compaction instant with zero perceived latency. Supports PDF-heavy conversations. Plan mode state and custom titles preserved after compaction. v2.1.70 improvement: images retained in summary requests for prompt cache reuse — faster and cheaper.

4.title: Partial Summary
4.desc: "Summarize from here" — select a starting point in the message picker to summarize only part of the conversation. No need to compact the entire context — precise control at your fingertips.

5.title: Todo List
5.desc: Built-in Todo list introduced in v0.2.93 to help Claude keep tasks organized. Upgraded in v2.1.16 to a full task management system with dependency tracking. /todos to view current tasks.

0.title: Deep Git & PR Integration
0.desc: Prompt footer shows real-time PR status for the current branch (approved/changes requested/pending/draft) with colored dots and clickable links. Sessions auto-associate with PRs. Teleport transfers sessions from the web to local and automatically sets the upstream branch.

1.title: Plan Mode — Think Before You Act
1.desc: Thinking mode arrived in v0.2.44 (say "think" to start thinking, "ultrathink" for deeper reasoning). The Plan sub-agent landed in v2.0.28. In v2.1.72, /plan can take the task description directly — for example /plan fix the auth bug — so planning starts the moment the idea lands. In VS Code, plan preview auto-updates and supports comment feedback. Shift+Tab quickly toggles auto-accept when it is time to let Claude run with it.

2.title: Web Search & Network Tools
2.desc: Web search introduced in v0.2.105. Network commands like curl enabled in v0.2.70. Claude can execute multiple web queries in parallel. URL fetch tool added in v0.2.53. Search results consider the current date.

3.title: Smart PDF Reading
3.desc: PDF reading first supported in v1.0.58. Read tool supports pages: "1-5" parameter for specific pages. Large PDFs (>10 pages) return lightweight references when @-mentioned. Limited to 100 pages, 20MB.

4.title: LSP Code Intelligence
4.desc: LSP tools introduced in v2.0.74: go-to-definition, find references, hover documentation. TypeScript and Python LSP plugins. findReferences automatically excludes gitignored files. Supports startupTimeout configuration.

5.title: SDK & Non-Interactive Mode
5.desc: TypeScript SDK (v1.0.23) and Python SDK (v1.0.23). -p mode supports streaming output (stream-json). --max-budget-usd for budget control. Supports custom tool callbacks.

0.title: Full MCP Protocol Support
0.desc: Project scope .mcp.json arrived in v0.2.50. Streamable HTTP and SSE + OAuth landed in v1.0.27. Auto-search mode (auto:N syntax) shipped in v2.1.7. Claude Code supports resource_link, structuredContent, and tool annotations. In v2.1.69, MCP tools returning binary content (PDF/Office/audio) started saving files to disk instead of dumping base64. In v2.1.72, tool search was fixed to keep working with ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL as long as ENABLE_TOOL_SEARCH is set.

1.title: OAuth Authentication Enhancements
1.desc: Supports OAuth Authorization Server discovery, step-up auth, and discovery caching. Pre-configured OAuth credentials (--client-id). Supports claude.ai MCP connectors (disable via ENABLE_CLAUDEAI_MCP_SERVERS=false). Fixed multi-instance token refresh race condition. v2.1.63 adds manual URL paste fallback when localhost redirect fails. v2.1.69 adds oauth.authServerMetadataUrl for custom OAuth metadata discovery URL.

2.title: Plugin System
2.desc: Plugin system released in v2.0.12. /plugin install with git branch/tag syntax support. Custom npm registries. Plugins can provide commands, agents, hooks, MCP servers, and output styles. v2.1.69 adds /reload-plugins hot reload, git-subdir plugin source type, and pluginTrustMessage for enterprise trust messaging. v2.1.72 supports marketplace git URLs without .git suffix (Azure DevOps, AWS CodeCommit compatible), and fixes multiple plugin installation issues on Windows OneDrive, marketplace scope conflicts, etc.

3.title: Skills & Custom Commands
3.desc: Custom slash commands (.claude/commands/) introduced in v0.2.31. Claude Skills introduced in v2.0.20. v2.1.3 merged slash commands with skills. SlashCommand tool lets Claude invoke your commands. v2.1.69 adds ${CLAUDE_SKILL_DIR} variable to reference a skill's own directory, plus the /claude-api built-in skill.

4.title: Hooks System
4.desc: Hooks released in v1.0.38. PreToolUse / PostToolUse / Stop / SessionStart / SessionEnd / ConfigChange / WorktreeCreate / TeammateIdle / TaskCompleted / PermissionRequest / UserPromptSubmit / InstructionsLoaded / Setup — complete lifecycle hooks. v2.1.63 adds HTTP hooks with POST JSON to URL and JSON response support. v2.1.69 adds InstructionsLoaded hook and agent_id/agent_type fields in hook events.

5.title: Remote Control & Teleport
5.desc: claude remote-control enables local environment serving for all users. v2.1.69 supports optional name parameter (/remote-control My Project or --name "My Project") to display custom titles in claude.ai/code. Teleport transfers sessions from the web to local. Claude in Chrome integrates browser control.

0.title: Themes & Syntax Highlighting
0.desc: /theme opens the theme picker directly. v2.0.10 rewrote the terminal renderer for a silky-smooth UI. Diff highlighting works correctly in multi-line constructs. v0.2.30 ANSI color themes compatible with more terminals.

1.title: Vim Mode & Shortcuts
1.desc: Vim bindings introduced in v0.2.34. Supports c, f/F, t/T motions. Custom keyboard shortcuts (/keybindings) in v2.1.18. Ctrl+R for history search. Ctrl+Z to suspend (fg to resume).

2.title: CJK & Internationalization
2.desc: IME input support (Chinese/Japanese/Korean) fixed in v0.2.68. CJK wide character layout renders correctly. Japanese IME full-width space/number input works. Thai/Lao vowel rendering fixed. Non-Latin script word deletion and navigation fixed. v2.1.70 fixes Windows/WSL clipboard corrupting CJK and emoji text, switching to PowerShell Set-Clipboard.

3.title: @ File Mentions & Images
3.desc: @ file mentions and image drag-and-drop/paste introduced in v0.2.75. MCP resource @ mentions supported. Folder click navigation. Anchor fragments (@README.md#installation). Index pre-warming at startup.

4.title: Terminal Compatibility
4.desc: /terminal-setup supports Kitty, Alacritty, Zed, Warp, WezTerm, iTerm2, and Ghostty. Full Kitty keyboard protocol support. OSC 8 hyperlinks clickable across lines. OSC 9;4 progress bar configurable. v2.1.69 adds numpad digit support. v2.1.70 fixes Enter inserting newlines instead of submitting on slow SSH, and fixes Shift+Enter issues in Ghostty over SSH.

5.title: Real-Time Interaction
5.desc: Real-time steering arrived in v0.2.108 so you can guide Claude while it is working. Message queuing landed in v0.2.75 so Enter can line up follow-up prompts. /copy interactively selects code blocks, and from v2.1.72 you can press w to write the focused selection straight to a file — especially handy over SSH or inside tmux. Prompt suggestions keep the next move obvious.

6.title: Voice Input
6.desc: Hold spacebar to start voice input (v2.1.71 added the voice:pushToTalk custom key binding). v2.1.69 supports 20 languages, including Chinese. v2.1.71 fixed the 5–8 second startup freeze caused by voice mode. v2.1.72 further improves transcription accuracy for repo names and dev terms like regex, OAuth, and JSON. When you do not feel like typing, just talk.

7.title: Bash Command Auto-Approval — More Commands Go Visa-Free
7.desc: v2.1.71 added fmt, seq, printf, test, expr to the allowlist; v2.1.72 continues expanding with lsof, pgrep, tput, ss, fd, and fdfind. High-frequency read-only operations like checking ports, finding processes, and searching files no longer prompt for confirmation — smoother daily development experience.

0.title: Sandbox Mode — AI Writes Code, but Runs in a Cage
0.desc: Linux & Mac sandbox mode released in v2.0.24. Blocks writes to .claude/skills directory. HTTP hooks enforce domain allowlists. Heredoc parsing prevents command injection. v2.1.69 adds sandbox.enableWeakerNetworkIsolation (macOS) to let Go programs like gh and gcloud verify TLS in proxy environments — balancing security with usability. v2.1.72: CLAUDE.md HTML comments (<!-- ... -->) are now hidden from Claude when auto-injected but remain visible via Read tool — handy for writing human-only notes in project rules.

1.title: Permission System
1.desc: /approved-tools introduced in v0.2.26. Upgraded to /permissions for full permission management in v1.0.72. Supports wildcards like mcp__server__*. Smart "always allow" prefix matching. Bash permission classifier validates matching descriptions.

2.title: Security Vulnerability Fixes
2.desc: Fixed wildcard permission rules matching shell operators. Fixed shell line continuation permission bypass. Fixed command injection in bash command handling. Fixed HTTP hooks environment variable interpolation. Fixed Bash tool permission check prefix matching bypass.

3.title: Enterprise Security
3.desc: Shared project permission rules (.claude/settings.json) introduced in v0.2.67. Enterprise managed settings in v2.0.68. ConfigChange hook for auditing. Managed settings support macOS plist and Windows Registry. MCP allow/deny lists. v2.1.69 adds pluginTrustMessage managed setting, pathPattern regex matching for file/directory marketplace sources, and policy restrictions for Team plan OAuth users.

0.title: TypeScript & Python SDK
0.desc: TypeScript SDK (@anthropic-ai/claude-code) and Python SDK (claude-code-sdk) released in v1.0.23. Supports streaming output (stream-json), custom tool callbacks (canUseTool), request cancellation, and partial message streaming.

1.title: Non-Interactive Mode (-p)
1.desc: -p mode supports streaming JSON output. --max-budget-usd for budget control. --system-prompt / --system-prompt-file for custom system prompts (supports both interactive and print modes). --append-system-prompt for appending prompts. Subtask messages include parent_tool_use_id. v2.1.69 improves documentation to clarify that --append-system-prompt-file and --system-prompt-file both work in interactive mode.

2.title: Claude Code Desktop — Download and Go
2.desc: Claude Code for Desktop (claude.com/download) launched in v2.0.51. A standalone desktop app accessible even for those unfamiliar with the terminal. v2.1.69: macOS and Windows prompt first-time users to try the Desktop version. Claude in Chrome also integrates browser control — no IDE required.

3.title: OpenTelemetry Observability
3.desc: OTel logs support session ID, terminal.type, and language attributes. mTLS support for HTTP exporters. Active Time metrics. Speed attribute tracks fast mode. tool_decision events. ANTHROPIC_LOG=debug for debug logging.

4.title: Output Styles
4.desc: Output styles system released in v1.0.81 with built-in "Explanatory" and "Learning" educational styles. Supports custom styles and plugin-shared styles. keep-coding-instructions frontmatter option.

5.title: Bedrock / Vertex / Foundry
5.desc: Supports AWS Bedrock, Google Vertex AI, and Microsoft Foundry — three major cloud providers. Custom model aliases (ANTHROPIC_DEFAULT_SONNET_MODEL). Global endpoints. awsAuthRefresh for credential refresh. Structured outputs.

0.title: Startup Optimization & Token Savings
0.desc: Deferred SessionStart hook execution (-500ms). Headless mode defers Yoga WASM and UI imports. Cached MCP auth failures to avoid repeated connections. v2.1.70 defers Yoga WASM preload saving ~16MB base memory. v2.1.71 defers native image processor loading until first use, fixes voice mode CoreAudio 5–8 second blocking, and fixes UI freeze from multiple proxies refreshing OAuth simultaneously. v2.1.72 fixes prompt cache invalidation in SDK query() calls, reducing input token costs up to 12x; bash command parsing switches to native module for faster init with no memory leak; bundle size reduced by ~510KB.

1.title: Memory Optimization
1.desc: Fixed 30+ memory leaks: tree-sitter parse trees, LSP diagnostic data, TaskOutput, CircularBuffer, ChildProcess references, Yoga WASM linear memory, file history snapshots, MCP server fetch cache, git root detection cache, JSON parse cache, WebSocket listeners, IDE host IP detection cache, bridge polling loop listeners, React Compiler memoCache, REPL render scopes, and more. v2.1.70 improves compaction to preserve images for prompt cache reuse, with 600 tokens/resume reduction (skill listings no longer re-injected on --resume).

2.title: Rendering Optimization
2.desc: React Compiler improves UI rendering performance (v2.1.69 further optimized in native binary). v2.1.70 reduces prompt input re-renders by ~74%, with spinner animation loops isolated to reduce rendering and CPU overhead. Fixed macOS OAuth MCP servers causing UI frame drops. Remote Control /poll frequency reduced to once every 10 minutes (when connected), ~300x reduction in server load.

0.title: Windows
0.desc: Native Windows support first added in v1.0.51 (requires Git for Windows). ARM64 native binary. winget installation. Fixed \r\n rendering, BashTool EINVAL, process spawn crashes, CWD temp file accumulation, and drive letter casing issues. Hooks execute via Git Bash. v2.1.70 fixes clipboard corrupting CJK/emoji text (switched to PowerShell Set-Clipboard) and extra windows when launching VS Code integrated terminal.

1.title: macOS
1.desc: Fixed sandbox temp file write permissions. Fixed read-only git commands triggering FSEvents loops. Option-as-Meta terminal-specific instructions. Code signing warning fixes.

2.title: Linux
2.desc: Fixed native module loading on glibc < 2.30 systems (RHEL 8). Fixed WebAssembly interpreter crashes. Agents load correctly on NFS/FUSE filesystems. Support for processors without AVX instructions. Alpine/musl distribution support. Wayland image paste.

3.title: WSL2
3.desc: Fixed image paste not working when Windows copies images in BMP format. Suggests using scp for pasting images over SSH.

4.title: Shell Compatibility
4.desc: Supports bash and zsh. MSYS2/Cygwin shell output handled correctly. .bashrc file compatibility. Double-underscore prefixed shell functions preserved across sessions. Zsh heredoc works correctly in sandbox.

5.title: Installation Methods
5.desc: npm (deprecated, migration recommended), winget, claude install for native installation. /doctor shows update channel and available versions. Correctly identifies mise/asdf-managed installations. Supports Bedrock, Vertex, and Foundry API providers.

0.title: Native VS Code Extension — Use Claude Without Opening a Terminal
0.desc: Brand new native VS Code extension released in v2.0.0. Supports secondary sidebar (VS Code 1.97+). Streaming messages display in real-time. Drag and drop files/folders into the chat window for direct referencing. v2.1.70 adds spark icon in the activity bar listing all sessions, with sessions opening as full editor tabs — bringing the experience closer to native IDE functionality.

1.title: Remote Sessions & Plugin Management
1.desc: OAuth users can browse and resume claude.ai remote sessions. Session selector shows git branch and message count. Direct plugin management. Permission target selections persist across sessions.

2.title: Plan Preview & Full Markdown View
2.desc: Plan preview auto-updates as Claude iterates. v2.1.70 adds a full Markdown document view with comment support for feedback. Comments enabled only when the plan is ready for review. Preview stays open on rejection. Code block copy button. Diff tab support.

3.title: Native MCP Server Management
3.desc: v2.1.70 adds a native MCP server management dialog. Use /mcp in the chat panel to enable/disable servers, reconnect, and manage OAuth authentication — no need to switch to the terminal.

4.title: Python Environment & Configuration
4.desc: Auto-activates Python virtual environments. claudeCode.usePythonEnvironment setting. Supports chat.fontSize/fontFamily customization. respectGitIgnore configuration. Initial permission mode settings.

5.title: Session Management
5.desc: Session fork and rewind features. /usage and /extra-usage commands. Multi-line input (Shift+Enter). Claude in Chrome integration. Tab icon badges show pending permissions and unread completions. v2.1.63 adds session rename and delete actions. v2.1.69 compaction displays as a collapsible "Compacted chat" card; permission mode selector respects disableBypassPermissionsMode setting.

6.title: Stability Fixes & v2.1.72 Enhancements
6.desc: Fixed extension crashes on Windows, terminal scroll-to-top regression, duplicate sessions, OAuth token expiry 401 errors, sidebar view container startup race conditions, and Windows ARM64 compatibility. v2.1.70 fixes RTL text (Arabic, Hebrew, Persian) rendering reversed in the chat panel. v2.1.72 adds effort level indicator on the input border (see current reasoning level at a glance) and vscode://anthropic.claude-code/open URI handler with optional prompt and session query parameters for programmatic tab opening.

0.desc: claude auth login/status/logout Auth management. claude agents List agents. claude remote-control Remote control. claude doctor Diagnostics. claude mcp add/list/get/serve MCP management. claude plugin install/validate/browse Plugin management. claude install Native install. claude --teleport Teleport from web.

1.desc: CLAUDE_CODE_SIMPLE Minimal mode. CLAUDE_CODE_TMPDIR Custom temp dir. CLAUDE_CODE_SHELL Override shell detection. CLAUDE_CODE_DISABLE_BACKGROUND_TASKS Disable background tasks. CLAUDE_CODE_DISABLE_1M_CONTEXT Disable 1M context. CLAUDE_CODE_DISABLE_GIT_INSTRUCTIONS Remove built-in Git instructions. CLAUDE_CODE_DISABLE_CRON Immediately stop scheduled cron jobs mid-session. ENABLE_CLAUDEAI_MCP_SERVERS=false Disable claude.ai MCP servers. CLAUDE_CODE_PLUGIN_GIT_TIMEOUT_MS Plugin git timeout. CLAUDE_CODE_ACCOUNT_UUID/CLAUDE_CODE_USER_EMAIL/CLAUDE_CODE_ORGANIZATION_UUID SDK account info. BASH_DEFAULT_TIMEOUT_MS Bash timeout. ANTHROPIC_LOG=debug Debug logging.

2.desc: Create markdown files in .claude/commands/ to define custom commands. Supports $ARGUMENTS parameters. /keybindings Custom keyboard shortcuts. /statusline Custom status line. settings.json changes take effect immediately without restart.

models.title: Scenario: Which Model Should I Choose?
models.subtitle: Switch flexibly based on task type

items.0: Just inherited a monorepo and can't figure out what some code does? Opus 4.6 + 1M context — view the entire module at once and get a crystal-clear explanation
items.1: Writing daily business logic and can't wait for deep reasoning? /fast to switch to Fast Mode — same model, faster results
items.2: Worker nodes in a multi-agent workflow that don't need a flagship model? Sonnet 4.6 delivers the best value
items.3: "Where is this function defined?" — The Haiku 4.5-powered Explore sub-agent searches your entire codebase in seconds
items.4: Say "ultrathink" to trigger high-effort reasoning; in v2.1.72, effort became a simple ○ ◐ ● scale, so with /effort you can instantly tell whether Claude is staying light or thinking hard

agents.title: Scenario: Refactoring a Large Module
agents.subtitle: Let multiple agents each handle their own part

items.0: You say "refactor the auth module" and the main agent auto-decomposes: security audit, performance analysis, and test coverage — a three-part combo
items.1: Three teammates each in their own tmux pane, working in isolated worktrees without stepping on each other
items.2: Done? The TeammateIdle hook auto-notifies you "security audit agent completed" with results rendered in rich Markdown — clear at a glance
items.3: Want to check what a specific agent is doing? <kbd>Shift+Down</kbd> to jump to its panel
items.4: Long-term collaborating agents can use memory frontmatter for persistent preferences — they hit the ground running on the next invocation

sessions.title: Scenario: Picking Up Monday Where Friday Left Off
sessions.subtitle: Sessions and memory seamlessly carry over — zero context lost

items.0: You coded all Friday afternoon — Claude has already memorized your naming conventions, project architecture, and even your preferred error handling patterns
items.1: Monday morning, claude --resume — search by branch name to find your last session, with your Friday final message still in the input
items.2: After an hour the context is filling up? /compact smartly compresses it — plan mode state, session title, even images are all preserved
items.3: Want to try a different approach? /fork branches off to explore, leaving the original conversation untouched
items.4: PR merged? Next time use --from-pr #123 to restore the relevant context — no need to re-explain the project background

devflow.title: Scenario: Day One at a New Job — Taking Over an Unfamiliar Project and Submitting a PR
devflow.subtitle: From clueless to committed — Claude guides you the whole way

items.0: The project has a 30-page architecture PDF? Just @-mention it — Claude smartly reads the key sections without stuffing all 30 pages into context
items.1: Code scattered across multiple repos? --add-dir adds other directories — cross-project code references without manual copy-paste
items.2: "What does this util function actually do?" — LSP tools jump to definition with one click, hover for docs — 10x faster than digging through source
items.3: Start in plan mode to talk through the approach — or jump straight in with /plan fix the auth bug. Once the plan looks good, Claude writes the code and you just review
items.4: Done writing? The prompt footer shows PR status in real-time: pending → approved → merged, with clickable links to GitHub

mcp.title: Scenario: Connecting Your Team's Toolchain with MCP
mcp.subtitle: Code done → auto-notify Slack, auto-create PR, auto-query database

items.0: Configure a Slack MCP server — after Claude finishes coding, it auto-posts to the channel: "PR #456 created, please review"
items.1: With a database MCP connector set up, just ask Claude "how many active users in production?" — it writes the SQL, runs the query, and returns results
items.2: Configured 20 MCP tools and it's too many? Auto-search mode only loads them when needed, saving precious context window space
items.3: PostToolUse hook auto-formats code and runs lint checks; ConfigChange hook audits every config change — enterprise compliance, painlessly
items.4: MCP tool returned a PDF or Excel file? Automatically saved to disk instead of dumping base64 gibberish into the conversation

terminal.title: Scenario: Writing Code on a Remote Server via SSH
terminal.subtitle: CJK input, themes, and shortcuts — all set up in one go

items.0: SSH into a remote server, /theme to pick a nice dark theme — diff highlighting and syntax coloring auto-adapt
items.1: Enable Vim mode — <kbd>f</kbd>/<kbd>t</kbd> for quick jumps, <kbd>Ctrl+R</kbd> for history search — fingers never leave the home row
items.2: Switch to a CJK input method for Chinese comments? Full-width spaces and wide character layouts all align correctly
items.3: Claude is running a long task? No need to wait — type a message to steer its direction, with real-time steering taking effect instantly
items.4: Too many code blocks in the response? /copy opens an interactive selector, and over SSH/tmux you can hit w to write the focused block straight to a file without touching the clipboard
items.5: Hold spacebar for voice input (20 languages supported), and repo names plus terms like OAuth and JSON now transcribe more accurately when you would rather speak than type

sdk.title: Scenario: Integrating Claude Code into Your Company's Release Pipeline
sdk.subtitle: SDK + OTel + Bedrock — enterprise-grade workflows

items.0: Python SDK calls Claude Code to auto-review every PR — --max-budget-usd 0.5 keeps per-review costs under control
items.1: In the CI pipeline, -p --output-format=stream-json for streaming output — review progress written to CI logs in real-time
items.2: OTel metrics exported to Grafana — see Claude's call volume, response times, and token consumption trends at a glance
items.3: Deployed in your company's AWS environment? Bedrock provider + awsAuthRefresh auto-refreshes credentials — secure and compliant
items.4: Custom Output Style unifies output format — no matter who triggers the review, the output looks exactly the same

performance.title: Scenario: An 8-Hour Marathon Coding Session
performance.subtitle: Running from morning to night without restarting — still silky smooth

items.0: The session you started in the morning is still going in the afternoon — internal caches are auto-cleared after compaction, and message history is auto-trimmed after agent tasks complete
items.1: A tool returned 80K characters of logs? Automatically persisted to disk — not eating up your precious context window
items.2: tree-sitter parsers periodically reset to prevent WASM memory leaks; React Compiler optimizes UI rendering — no lag even after hours
items.3: Running batch reviews via SDK? After the prompt cache fix, input token costs drop up to 12x — same work, less money
items.4: Typing responsiveness stays smooth — spinner animations are isolated in their own loop; bash parsing switched to native module for faster and more stable performance

platform.title: Scenario: Five Team Members, Three Operating Systems
platform.subtitle: Windows + macOS + Linux — unified experience

items.0: The Windows teammate runs winget install Anthropic.ClaudeCode — one-click install, runs on Surface Pro ARM64 too
items.1: The macOS teammate runs claude install — done. Sandbox mode auto-adapts temp directories, no extra config needed
items.2: The ops teammate running RHEL 8 (glibc < 2.30)? No problem — Alpine/musl is supported too
items.3: WSL2 user pasting images copied from Windows? BMP format compatibility is fixed — just paste
items.4: Everyone runs /doctor for a one-click health check — mise/asdf-managed installations are correctly detected, no manual path troubleshooting needed

keyboard_descs.5: Exit bash mode / Clear input line

slash_descs.2: Run commands on a timed loop (e.g., /loop 5m check deploy)
slash_descs.3: Enter plan mode (supports passing the task description directly)
slash_descs.4: Tune reasoning effort (use /effort auto to reset)
slash_descs.6: Interactively select and copy code blocks (supports w to write directly to a file)
slash_descs.11: Rename session (works while Claude is processing)
slash_descs.14: Configuration (Esc cancels, Enter saves, Space toggles)
slash_descs.38: Set session color (use default or reset to restore)

0.desc: Early foundational features: fuzzy command matching, Vim bindings, custom slash commands, MCP project scope, auto-compaction, thinking mode, @ file mentions, image paste, MCP SSE, Web search.

1.desc: Real-time steering, message queuing, network tools (curl), Claude Max subscription support, --continue/--resume session recovery, Todo list, Web search.

2.desc: Claude Code GA official release! Sonnet 4 + Opus 4 models. Native VS Code extension. /rewind, /usage, Ctrl+R history search. SDK upgraded to Claude Agent SDK.

3.desc: TypeScript/Python SDK. Hooks system released. Custom Agents. /permissions management. Background commands (Ctrl+B). Opus Plan Mode. MCP multi-config files. Alpine/musl support.

4.desc: Output styles. PDF reading. /context command. /memory editing. Bedrock mTLS. Opus 4.1 upgrade. Ctrl+R history search enhancements.

5.desc: 2.0 major release! Brand new native VS Code extension. New UI. /rewind conversation rollback. /usage quota check. Tab to toggle thinking. Dynamic sub-agent addition.

6.desc: Opus 4.5 released! Claude Code Desktop. Plan Mode enhancements. Foundry support. PermissionRequest hook. Background agents. Custom agent configuration.

7.desc: Pro users get Opus 4.5. Plugin system released. Claude in Chrome. LSP code intelligence. Terminal renderer rewrite. Haiku 4.5 + Explore sub-agent.

8.desc: Entering the 2.1 era. Skill hot reload. Slash commands merged with skills. Permission rule detection. winget installation support.

9.desc: MCP tool auto-search mode. Hooks enhancements. Bash history completion. Task management system. Custom keyboard shortcuts. React Compiler rendering optimization.

10.desc: PR status indicator. PDF paginated reading. /debug command. --from-pr session association. --resume memory reduced by 68%.

11.desc: Opus 4.6 officially released! Agent Teams research preview. Auto-memory feature. Partial conversation summary.

12.desc: Agent Teams refinements (teammate messages, memory, hooks). Fast Mode supports Opus 4.6.

13.desc: Terminal rendering performance optimization. Windows ARM64 native support. claude auth subcommand. Heredoc command injection protection.

14.desc: Sonnet 4.6 launches. Agent worktree isolation. 10+ memory leak fixes. CJK layout fixes. 60+ bug fixes (v2.1.47 — the largest single update ever).

15.desc: Remote Control subcommand. Custom npm registries. HTTP hooks sandbox routing. Windows stability fixes.

16.desc: Remote Control expanded to more users. Auto-memory upgraded with /memory management. New /copy interactive code block selection. Smart permission prefix for compound commands. Multi-instance concurrency safety fixes. Windows config file concurrent write fixes.

17.desc: Opus 4.6 defaults to medium effort (Max/Team subscriptions). Reintroduced ultrathink keyword. Removed Opus 4/4.1; Sonnet 4.5 auto-migrated to 4.6. New /claude-api skill. Voice STT expanded to 20 languages. New /simplify and /batch built-in skills. HTTP hooks (POST JSON). /reload-plugins hot reload. InstructionsLoaded hook. MCP binary content auto-save. Project config and auto-memory shared across git worktrees. Numerous memory leak fixes and performance optimizations.

18.desc: VS Code major upgrade: spark icon session list, Plan full Markdown view with comments, native MCP server management dialog. Prompt re-render reduced by 74%. Compaction preserves images for prompt cache reuse. Remote Control /poll load reduced by 300x. /rename works during processing. /color supports reset. Fixed third-party gateway API 400 errors, Windows/WSL clipboard CJK/emoji corruption, slow SSH Enter issues, and more.

19.desc: v2.1.71 added /loop and cron scheduling tools; v2.1.72 followed with /plan taking a task description, /copy supporting w to write directly to files, effort simplified to ○ ◐ ●, ExitWorktree closing the loop on isolated sessions, and tool search restored for ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL setups. Add a big batch of exit, permissions, background-task, and voice fixes, and the whole workflow feels more polished.

20.desc: Added modelOverrides setting to map model picker entries to custom provider model IDs (e.g. Bedrock inference profile ARNs). Default Opus model on Bedrock/Vertex/Foundry upgraded to Opus 4.6. Fixed 100% CPU loops from permission prompts, deadlocks from bulk skill file changes, multi-session Bash output loss, subagent model downgrades on cloud providers. Deprecated /output-style (use /config). Up arrow after interrupt now restores prompt and rewinds in one step.

21.desc: /context command now shows actionable optimization suggestions (context bottleneck diagnostics). autoMemoryDirectory for custom auto-memory storage. Fixed streaming API memory leak (unbounded RSS growth), MCP OAuth callback port conflicts, RTL text on Windows Terminal, LSP Windows URI format errors. --plugin-dir local copies now override marketplace plugins. VS Code: fixed Untitled session delete, improved scroll wheel responsiveness.

22.desc: 1M context window for Opus 4.6 now default for Max/Team/Enterprise. New /color command to set prompt bar color. /rename shows session name on prompt bar. Memory files include last-modified timestamps. Hook source displayed in permission prompts. Fixed voice mode, token over-counting issues.

23.desc: MCP elicitation support — MCP servers can pop up interactive dialogs mid-task. New Elicitation/ElicitationResult/PostCompact hooks. -n/--name flag names sessions at startup. worktree.sparsePaths for sparse checkout in large monorepos. /effort slash command adjusts reasoning intensity. Fixed deferred tool schema loss, plan mode re-approval, auto-compaction infinite retry. Improved worktree startup performance and stale worktree cleanup.
